family.reason: Remote ferry journey, challenging terrain, limited family infrastructure

couples.reason: Wilderness, hot springs, mountain solitude — unique romantic atmosphere

nightlife.reason: Almost no nightlife — a kafeneio in Hora is the extent of evening entertainment

food.reason: Mountain goat, fresh fish, wild herb honey, local tsipouro

beaches.reason: Wild pebble beaches — decent but not the reason to visit

walking.reason: Mount Fengari, Fonias gorge, Vatos pools, Sanctuary valley — exceptional hiking

quick_summary.en: The sacred mountain island of the North Aegean — Sanctuary of the Great Gods, Winged Victory of Samothrace, Mount Fengari (1,611m), Fonias waterfalls, natural hot springs, and one of the most untamed natural environments in Greece.
quick_summary.el: Το ιερό βουνό του Βορείου Αιγαίου — Ιερό των Μεγάλων Θεών, Νίκη της Σαμοθράκης, όρος Φεγγάρι (1.611μ), καταρράκτες Φονιά, ιαματικές πηγές και ένα από τα πιο άγρια φυσικά περιβάλλοντα της Ελλάδας.

pros: Sanctuary of the Great Gods — one of the most important ancient sites in Greece, almost always empty
Mount Fengari — highest peak in the Aegean (1,611m), extraordinary summit views
Fonias River gorge and 12m waterfall — finest fresh-water swimming in the Aegean
Therma natural hot springs — free, used since antiquity, set among enormous plane trees
Zero mass tourism — no airport, no resorts, no beach clubs
Hiking paradise — from easy waterfall walks to demanding mountain ascents
cons: Remote and difficult to reach — ferry-only, no airport
Limited accommodation options — books out fully in peak season
Not a beach destination — beaches are wild pebble coves
Car essential for exploration — limited public transport
Weather-dependent activities — river gorges dangerous after rain
insider_tips: Start the Fonias gorge walk before 9am to have the waterfall pool to yourself
Visit the Sanctuary museum first (60–90min), then the site — the context transforms the ruins
The Therma hot springs are best at sunset when the air cools and the steam rises
Book ferry and accommodation at least two months ahead for July–August
Pack trail shoes, warm layers, headlamp and 2L water for the Fengari ascent
September is the finest month — crowds gone, sea warm, mountain clear
mistakes_to_avoid: Coming expecting a beach holiday — the beaches are decent but secondary
Arriving without accommodation booked in high season
Entering the Fonias gorge within 24 hours of rain — flash floods are dangerous
Treating the Sanctuary as a quick stop — it deserves a full half-day
Underestimating the Fengari ascent — 8–10 hours, 1,400m elevation gain
Skipping the Hora — the medieval hilltop capital is one of the finest in the North Aegean
